name: taches-principled
description: Principle-based plugin ecosystem for Claude Code — planning, execution, evaluation, development skills, and an optimal MCP wrapper for the Claude Code CLI.

plugins.name: core-principled
plugins.description: Principle-based Claude Code plugin for the full development lifecycle — planning, execution, authoring, code review, root-cause analysis, continuous improvement, ideation, spec-driven development, documentation, code refinement, multi-agent patterns, tool design, security auditing, test strategy, project maintenance (plan archival and memory hygiene), code structure (architecture, transparency, API design, idiomatic naming), tool-agnostic web search best practices (reformulation, source hierarchy, verification), and a live Claude Code / Agent SDK / Claude API documentation lookup agent (tp-cc-docs) backed by the official llms.txt index.

plugins.name: tp-sadd
plugins.description: Structured agent-driven development with multi-pattern evaluation — judge, sequential, parallel, competitive, and tree-of-thoughts execution with meta-judge verification.

plugins.name: tp-fpf
plugins.description: First Principles Framework — structured reasoning with hypothesis generation, evidence validation, and auditable decision records.

plugins.name: tp-git
plugins.description: Git workflow automation — conventional commits, PR creation, issue analysis, git notes, worktrees, and inline PR review comments.

plugins.name: tp-session-audit
plugins.description: Session analytics — CAPTURE (headless behavioral artifact collection), INSPECT (structured data extraction), REVIEW (behavioral anti-pattern diagnosis), ISSUE (sanitized GitHub issue reporting). One hub skill, four modes.

plugins.name: claude-cli-wrapper
plugins.description: Optimal MCP wrapper for the Claude Code CLI — single Rust binary exposing six tools (claude_execute, claude_session, claude_context, claude_review, claude_agent, claude_config) over both MCP stdio and a `mcp-tool` script fallback. Flat snake_case parameter surfaces, action-enum discriminators for lifecycle tools, pass-through for complex JSON, UUID session_id validation, and enum-bounded mode/effort/permission. Cross-platform launcher at `bin/claude-cli-wrapper` resolves to a per-host prebuilt (Apple Silicon), cached local build, or freshly compiled binary on first use (~2-3 min). Ideal for any agent that needs to drive Claude Code programmatically.

plugins.name: tp-rust
plugins.description: Principled Rust project skills — scaffold single crates and multi-crate workspaces with edition 2024 / MSRV 1.81 defaults, set up the CI + clippy + nextest + cargo-deny quality pipeline, and ship releases with version policy, changelog generation, publishing, and supply-chain hardening. Four skills covering the full Rust lifecycle: rust-scaffold, rust-workspace, rust-quality, rust-release.

plugins.name: tp-mcp
plugins.description: Principled MCP (Model Context Protocol) server skills — design (equilibrated recursivity, tool decomposition, pass-through, output contract, JSON-RPC error discipline, context budget, annotations, naming, capability negotiation, security), implement (rmcp + schemars + tokio, transport choice, stderr-only logging, error mapping, testing), and tool-surface authoring (JSON Schema 2020-12 constraints, additionalProperties:false discipline, discriminator enums, naming). Three skills covering the full MCP server lifecycle, plus the `claude-cli` skill in the existing `claude-cli-wrapper` plugin as a worked example of the 6-tool decomposition pattern.
